title.zh: 卷七十九 馮奉世傳
title.raw: 卷七十九 馮奉世傳 Volume 79: Feng Fengshi

sentences.zh: 卷七十九 馮奉世傳第四十九

translations.literal: Volume 79, Biography of Feng Fengshi, Number 49.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 79: Biography of Feng Fengshi, the forty-ninth.

sentences.zh: 馮奉世字子明，上黨潞人也，徙杜陵。

translations.literal: Feng Fengshi, courtesy name Ziming, was a man of Lu in Shangdang; his family moved to Duling.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fengshi, style Ziming, came from Lu in Shangdang and later settled at Duling.

sentences.zh: 其先馮亭，為韓上黨守。

translations.literal: His ancestor was Feng Ting, who was governor of Shangdang for Han.
translations.idiomatic: His forebear Feng Ting had held Shangdang for the state of Han.

sentences.zh: 秦攻上黨，絕太行道，韓不能守，馮亭乃入上黨城守於趙。

translations.literal: Qin attacked Shangdang, cut the Taihang road, and Han could not hold it; Feng Ting thereupon entered the city of Shangdang and held it for Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin severed the Taihang passes and Han could not defend the commandery, Feng Ting turned the territory over to Zhao and defended it in Zhao's name.

sentences.zh: 趙封馮亭為華陽君，與趙將括距秦，戰死於長平。

translations.literal: Zhao ennobled Feng Ting as lord of Huayang; with the Zhao general Kuo he resisted Qin and died in battle at Changping.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao enfeoffed him as lord of Huayang; he fought beside the general Zhao Kuo against Qin and fell at Changping.

sentences.zh: 宗族由是分散，或留潞，或在趙。

translations.literal: The lineage was thereby scattered; some remained in Lu, some were in Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: The clan scattered; one branch stayed in Lu, another in Zhao.

sentences.zh: 在趙者為官帥將，官帥將子為代相。

translations.literal: Those in Zhao became commanders of garrison officers; a son of a garrison commander became chancellor of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhao branch produced garrison commanders, and a commander's son rose to chancellor of Dai.

sentences.zh: 及秦滅六國，而馮亭之後馮毋擇、馮去疾、馮劫皆為秦將相焉。

translations.literal: When Qin extinguished the six states, descendants of Feng Ting—Feng Wuzhe, Feng Quji, and Feng Jie—all became generals and ministers of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin swallowed the rival kingdoms, Feng Ting's descendants Wuzhe, Quji, and Jie all served Qin as generals and ministers.

sentences.zh: 漢興，文帝時馮唐顯名，即代相子也。

translations.literal: When Han arose, in Emperor Wen's time Feng Tang became famous; he was the son of the chancellor of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Under Han, Feng Tang won renown under Emperor Wen—he was that chancellor of Dai's son.

sentences.zh: 至武帝末，奉世以良家子選為郎。

translations.literal: By the end of Emperor Wu's reign, Fengshi was selected as a gentleman from a good family.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Emperor Wu's reign he entered the corps of gentlemen cadets from a respectable household.

sentences.zh: 昭帝時，以功次補武安長。

translations.literal: In Emperor Zhao's time, by order of merit he was appointed magistrate of Wu'an.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Zhao he earned appointment as magistrate of Wu'an by seniority of service.

sentences.zh: 失官，年三十餘矣，乃學《春秋》涉大義，讀兵法明習，前將軍韓增奏以為軍司空令。

translations.literal: He lost office; already past thirty, he then studied the Spring and Autumn Annals and grasped its great principles, read military texts until expert, and the former general Han Zeng memorialized to appoint him director of the army works command.
translations.idiomatic: Stripped of rank past thirty, he mastered the Spring and Autumn, absorbed its large themes, and pored over strategy until the former general Han Zeng nominated him as director of the army works command.

sentences.zh: 本始中，從軍擊匈奴。

translations.literal: In the Benshi period he followed the army to strike the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: During Benshi he campaigned with the host against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 軍罷，復為郎。

translations.literal: When the army was disbanded, he again became a gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: After demobilization he returned to the gentleman corps.

sentences.zh: 先是時，漢數出使西域，多辱命不稱，或貪污，為外國所苦。

translations.literal: Earlier, Han had repeatedly sent envoys to the Western Regions; many disgraced their commissions or proved unworthy, some were corrupt, and the outer states suffered for it.
translations.idiomatic: Han envoys to the Western Regions had often failed or grown rich at local expense, to the outer states' misery.

sentences.zh: 是時，烏孫大有擊匈奴之功，而西域諸國新輯，漢方善遇，欲以安之，選可使外國者。

translations.literal: At that time Wusun had achieved great merit in striking the Xiongnu, while the states of the Western Regions had newly been brought to order; Han was treating them generously and wished to pacify them, and therefore selected one who could be sent abroad.
translations.idiomatic: Wusun had lately bloodied the Xiongnu, the Tarim states were settling under Han favor, and the court wanted a trustworthy envoy.

sentences.zh: 前將軍增舉奉世以衛候使持節送大宛諸國客。

translations.literal: The former general Han Zeng recommended Fengshi as guard commandant with staff of authority to escort the guests of Ferghana and the other states.
translations.idiomatic: Han Zeng nominated him as guard commandant bearing the staff to convoy Ferghana's party and the rest.

sentences.zh: 至伊脩城，都尉宋將言莎車與旁國共攻殺漢所置莎車王萬年，並殺漢使者奚充國。

translations.literal: When he reached Yixiu city, commandant Song Jiang reported that Shache together with neighboring states had jointly attacked and killed Wannian, the king of Shache whom Han had installed, and had also killed Han's envoy Xi Chongguo.
translations.idiomatic: At Yixiu the local commandant Song Jiang warned that Shache and allies had murdered Wannian, Han's puppet king of Shache, and slaughtered envoy Xi Chongguo.

sentences.zh: 時，匈奴又發兵攻車師城，不能下而去。

translations.literal: At that time the Xiongnu had again sent troops to attack the city of Jushi but could not take it and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu had just besieged Jushi without success and marched away.

sentences.zh: 莎車遣使揚言北道諸國已屬匈奴矣，於是攻劫南道，與歃盟畔漢，從鄯善以西皆絕不通。

translations.literal: Shache sent envoys to boast that the states of the northern route already belonged to the Xiongnu; thereupon it raided the southern route, smeared blood in alliance, rebelled against Han, and from Shanshan westward all communication was cut.
translations.idiomatic: Shache's heralds claimed the northern track for the Xiongnu, then plundered the southern oaths, broke with Han, and sealed the road west of Shanshan.

sentences.zh: 都護鄭吉、校尉司馬意皆在北道諸國間。

translations.literal: The protector-general Zheng Ji and colonel Sima Yi were both among the states of the northern route.
translations.idiomatic: Protector-general Zheng Ji and colonel Sima Yi were tied up on the northern line.

sentences.zh: 奉世與其副嚴昌計，以為不亟擊之則莎車日強，其勢難制，必危西域。

translations.literal: Fengshi and his deputy Yan Chang planned, holding that unless they struck quickly Shache would grow stronger day by day, its power hard to control, and the Western Regions would surely be endangered.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi and deputy Yan Chang judged that delay would let Shache swell until the whole region slipped from Han control.

sentences.zh: 遂以節諭告諸國王，因發其兵，南北道合萬五千人進擊莎車，攻拔其城。

translations.literal: Thereupon with the staff he issued proclamations to the kings of the states and mobilized their troops; the northern and southern routes combined fifteen thousand men and advanced to attack Shache, storming and taking its city.
translations.idiomatic: He invoked the staff, rallied the kings, and raised fifteen thousand men from both routes to storm Shache and take its walls.

sentences.zh: 莎車王自殺，傳其首詣長安。

translations.literal: The king of Shache killed himself; his head was sent to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The Shache king committed suicide; his head went to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 諸國悉平，威振西域。

translations.literal: All the states were pacified; his might shook the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: The oases submitted; his name thundered across the Tarim.

sentences.zh: 奉世乃罷兵以聞。

translations.literal: Fengshi thereupon disbanded the troops and reported the matter.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi stood the army down and memorialized what he had done.

sentences.zh: 宣帝召見韓增，曰：「賀將軍所舉得其人。」

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan summoned Han Zeng and said, "We congratulate the general on recommending the right man."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan called in Han Zeng and said, "Your nomination was sound."

sentences.zh: 奉世遂西至大苑。

translations.literal: Fengshi thereupon proceeded west to Ferghana.
translations.idiomatic: He then pushed on west to Ferghana.

sentences.zh: 大苑聞其斬莎車王，敬之異於它使。

translations.literal: Ferghana, hearing that he had beheaded the king of Shache, honored him differently from other envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Ferghana, learning he had decapitated the Shache king, treated him with a respect no other envoy had known.

sentences.zh: 得其名馬象龍而還。

translations.literal: He obtained their famous horse "Elephant Dragon" and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He brought back the celebrated "Elephant Dragon" steed.

sentences.zh: 上甚說，下議封奉世。

translations.literal: The emperor was greatly pleased and sent the matter down for deliberation on enfeoffing Fengshi.
translations.idiomatic: The throne was delighted and ordered discussion of a fief for Fengshi.

sentences.zh: 丞相、將軍皆曰：「《春秋》之義，大夫出疆，有可以安國家，則顓之可也。

translations.literal: The chancellor and the generals all said, "By the meaning of the Spring and Autumn Annals, when a grandee goes beyond the frontier, if there is something by which the state can be secured, specialized authority is permissible.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor and generals cited the Annals: a minister beyond the border may act on his own authority if the realm is thereby secured.

sentences.zh: 奉世功傚尤著，宜加爵士之賞。」

translations.literal: Fengshi's merit and effect were especially conspicuous; he should receive reward of rank and fief."
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi's achievement stood out; he deserved noble rank and a fief."

sentences.zh: 少府蕭望之獨以奉世奉使有指，而擅矯制違命，發諸國兵，雖有功效，不可以為後法。

translations.literal: Junior privy treasurer Xiao Wangzhi alone held that Fengshi had gone on mission with definite orders, yet on his own forged an edict and disobeyed command in raising the troops of the states; though there was merit and effect, it cannot be taken as a precedent for later.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao Wangzhi alone argued that Fengshi had carried fixed orders yet counterfeited an imperial rescript and mobilized foreign troops without warrant—precedent-making even if successful.

sentences.zh: 即封奉世，開後奉使者利，以奉世為比，爭逐發兵，要功萬里之外，為國家生事於夷狄。

translations.literal: If Fengshi is enfeoffed, it opens the way for later envoys to profit, taking Fengshi as their model, competing to raise troops and seek merit beyond ten thousand li, thereby creating trouble for the state among the Yi and Di.
translations.idiomatic: Ennobling him would tempt every future envoy to raise armies for glory ten thousand li away and embroil the court in barbarian quarrels.

sentences.zh: 漸不可長，奉世不宜受封。

translations.literal: What gradually grows must not be encouraged; Fengshi should not receive a fief.
translations.idiomatic: Such habits must not be fed; Fengshi should not take a fief.

sentences.zh: 上善望之議，以奉世為光祿大夫、水衡都尉。

translations.literal: The emperor approved Wangzhi's proposal and appointed Fengshi grand counselor of the palace and superintendent of the palace parks.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor took Wangzhi's side, naming Fengshi grand counselor of the palace and superintendent of the palace parks instead.

sentences.zh: 元帝即位，為執金吾。

translations.literal: When Emperor Yuan ascended the throne, he became metropolitan commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Yuan he became metropolitan commandant.

sentences.zh: 上郡屬國歸義降胡萬餘人反去。

translations.literal: More than ten thousand surrendered Hu of the dependent state of Shang commandery rebelled and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Over ten thousand surrendered Xiongnu of Shang commandery's dependent state rose and bolted.

sentences.zh: 初，昭帝末，西河屬國胡伊酋若王亦將眾數千人畔，奉世輒持節將兵追擊。

translations.literal: Earlier, at the end of Emperor Zhao's reign, the Xiongnu noble Yijiuoru king of the dependent state of Xihe also led several thousand followers in rebellion; Fengshi immediately took the staff and led troops in pursuit.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Zhao's reign the Xihe dependent chieftain Yijiuoru had mutinied with thousands; Fengshi had seized the staff and run him down.

sentences.zh: 右將軍典屬國常惠薨，奉世代為右將軍典屬國，加諸吏之號。

translations.literal: When the right general and director of dependent states Chang Hui died, Fengshi replaced him as right general and director of dependent states, with the added title of various officials.
translations.idiomatic: When right general Chang Hui died, Fengshi succeeded him as right general and director of dependent states, with the collective title "various officials."

sentences.zh: 數歲，為光祿勳。

translations.literal: After several years he became superintendent of the household.
translations.idiomatic: A few years later he rose to superintendent of the household.

sentences.zh: 永光二年秋，隴西羌□姐旁種反，詔召丞相韋玄成、御史大夫鄭弘、大司馬車騎將軍王接、左將軍許嘉、右將軍奉世入議。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the second year of Yongguang, the Qiang of Longxi, the tribal confederation beside the Xianjie [one graph missing in the text], rebelled; an edict summoned Chancellor Wei Xuancheng, Imperial Counselor Zheng Hong, grand marshal and chariot-and-cavalry general Wang Jie, left general Xu Jia, and right general Fengshi to enter and deliberate.
translations.idiomatic: Yongguang 2 autumn: Longxi Qiang of the Xianjie confederation (the manuscript loses one character in the name) rose in revolt; the edict called Chancellor Wei Xuancheng, Counselor Zheng Hong, Grand Marshal Wang Jie, Left General Xu Jia, and Right General Fengshi to council.

sentences.zh: 是時，歲比不登，京師谷石二百餘，邊郡四百，關東五百。

translations.literal: At that time harvests had failed year after year; in the capital grain was over two hundred cash a bushel, on the border four hundred, east of the passes five hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Years of bad harvests had driven grain past two hundred cash a bushel in the capital, four hundred on the frontier, five hundred in the east.

sentences.zh: 四方饑饉，朝廷方以為憂，而遭羌變。

translations.literal: Famine was everywhere; the court was already anxious, and then came the Qiang uprising.
translations.idiomatic: Famine gripped the realm just as the court faced the Qiang crisis.

sentences.zh: 玄成等漠然莫有對者。

translations.literal: Xuancheng and the others were blank and none had an answer.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xuancheng and the rest sat mute.

sentences.zh: 奉世曰：「羌虜近在境內背畔，不以時誅，亡以威制遠蠻。

translations.literal: Fengshi said, "The Qiang raiders are close inside the borders in rebellion; unless they are executed in good time, there is no way to awe and control the distant Man.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi said, "Qiang traitors are inside our border; crush them now or distant tribes will never fear Han.

sentences.zh: 臣願帥師討之。」

translations.literal: Your servant begs to lead an army to punish them."
translations.idiomatic: I ask command to chastise them."

sentences.zh: 上問用兵之數，對曰：「臣聞善用兵者，役不再興，糧不三載，故師不久暴而天誅亟決。

translations.literal: The emperor asked the scale of employing troops; he replied, saying, "Your servant has heard that those skilled in using troops do not twice raise levies nor thrice transport grain; therefore the army is not long exposed and Heaven's punishment is quickly settled.
translations.idiomatic: Asked the needed force, he answered: "A master of war does not mobilize twice nor haul grain three times; the blow must be swift.

sentences.zh: 往者數不料敵，而師至於折傷；

translations.literal: In the past several times the enemy was misjudged and the army reached the point of being broken and wounded;
translations.idiomatic: Past campaigns misread the foe and paid in blood;

sentences.zh: 再三發軵，則曠日煩費，威武虧矣。

translations.literal: if repeatedly mobilized, days are wasted, expense mounts, and martial awesomeness is depleted.
translations.idiomatic: repeat the levy and costs soar while prestige drains away.

sentences.zh: 今反虜無慮三萬人，法當倍用六萬人。

translations.literal: Now the rebel bandits number on the order of thirty thousand; by rule one should double that and use sixty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: These rebels number perhaps thirty thousand; doctrine says double them—sixty thousand men.

sentences.zh: 然羌戎弓矛之兵耳，器不犀利，可用四萬人，一月足以決。」

translations.literal: Yet the Qiang Rong are bow-and-spear troops; their weapons are not keen; forty thousand men can be used, and in one month it is sufficient to decide the affair."
translations.idiomatic: They are only bowmen and spearmen with dull gear; forty thousand can finish the job in a month."

sentences.zh: 丞相、御史、兩將軍皆以為民方收斂時，未可多發；

translations.literal: The chancellor, imperial counselor, and both generals all held that the people were just in harvest season and many could not be sent;
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor, counselor, and both generals insisted the harvest forbade a large levy;

sentences.zh: 萬人屯守之，且足。

translations.literal: ten thousand men garrisoned on guard will for the moment suffice.
translations.idiomatic: ten thousand on the line would do for now.

sentences.zh: 奉世曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Fengshi said, "It will not do.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi said, "That will not serve.

sentences.zh: 天下被饑饉，士馬羸秏，守戰之備久廢不簡，夷狄皆有輕邊吏之心，而羌首難。

translations.literal: The empire suffers famine; soldiers and horses are wasted and thin; preparations for defense and battle have long been neglected and not drilled; the Yi and Di all harbor contempt for border officials in their hearts, and the Qiang chieftains are hard.
translations.idiomatic: The realm is starving, mounts are gaunt, garrisons are rusty, barbarians despise frontier officers, and the Qiang chiefs are bold.

sentences.zh: 今以萬人分屯數外，虜見兵少，必不畏懼，戰則挫兵病師，守則百姓不救。

translations.literal: Now if ten thousand men are divided to garrison several places, the raiders seeing few troops will surely not fear; if they fight, the troops will be broken and the army injured; if they defend, the common people will not be saved.
translations.idiomatic: Split ten thousand among several posts and the enemy will see weakness—attack and we shatter, stand fast and the people perish.

sentences.zh: 如此，怯弱之形見，羌人乘利，諸種並和，相扇而起，臣恐中國之役不得止於四萬，非財幣所能解也。

translations.literal: In this way a look of cowardice appears; the Qiang will seize the advantage, all kinds will join in harmony, fan one another, and rise together; your servant fears that service for the Middle Kingdom will not stop at forty thousand, nor can it be resolved with mere wealth and coin.
translations.idiomatic: They will scent fear, clans will league, and I fear the war will outgrow forty thousand men and outrun any treasury.

sentences.zh: 故少發師而曠日，與一舉而疾決，利害相萬也。」

translations.literal: Therefore to raise few troops and waste days versus to strike once and decide quickly—the benefit and harm are ten thousand apart."
translations.idiomatic: A small force that drags on is nothing like one sharp stroke—the difference is an order of magnitude."

sentences.zh: 固爭之，不能得。

translations.literal: He firmly disputed it but could not prevail.
translations.idiomatic: He argued hard and lost.

sentences.zh: 有詔益二千人。

translations.literal: There was an edict to add two thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: The edict added only two thousand men.

sentences.zh: 於是遣奉世將萬二千人騎，以將屯為名。

translations.literal: Thereupon Fengshi was dispatched leading twelve thousand foot and horse, in the name of commanding garrison troops.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi marched with twelve thousand foot and cavalry under the label of garrison command.

sentences.zh: 典屬國任立、護軍都尉韓昌為偏裨，到隴西，分屯三處。

translations.literal: Director of dependent states Ren Li and commandant of guards Han Chang served as wing commanders; reaching Longxi, they divided garrisons among three places.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Li, director of dependent states, and Han Chang, commandant of guards, were his lieutenants; in Longxi they split into three camps.

sentences.zh: 典屬國為右軍，屯白石；

translations.literal: The director of dependent states was the right army, garrisoned at Baishi;
translations.idiomatic: Ren Li took the right wing at Baishi;

sentences.zh: 護軍都尉為前軍，屯臨洮；

translations.literal: the commandant of guards was the forward army, garrisoned at Lintao;
translations.idiomatic: Han Chang the vanguard at Lintao;

sentences.zh: 奉世為中軍，屯首陽西極上。

translations.literal: Fengshi was the central army, garrisoned at the western extremity above Shouyang.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi held the center on the heights west of Shouyang.

sentences.zh: 前軍到降同阪，先遣校尉在前與羌爭地利，又別遣校尉救民於廣陽谷。

translations.literal: When the forward army reached the Jiangtong Slope, it first sent a colonel ahead to contest terrain with the Qiang, and separately sent a colonel to rescue commoners in the Guangyang ravine.
translations.idiomatic: The vanguard reached Jiangtong Slope, sent a colonel to fight for ground, and another to evacuate civilians from Guangyang gorge.

sentences.zh: 羌虜盛多，皆為所破，殺兩校尉。

translations.literal: The Qiang raiders were numerous in strength; all were broken by them, and the two colonels were killed.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang massed and overwhelmed both columns, killing the colonels.

sentences.zh: 奉世具上地形部眾多少之計，願益三萬六千人乃足以決事。

translations.literal: Fengshi fully reported upward the plan for terrain and the numbers of units, begging to add thirty-six thousand men and then it would be sufficient to settle the affair.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi mapped terrain and tallies, begging for thirty-six thousand more men to finish the campaign.

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子大為發兵六萬餘人，拜太常弋陽侯任千秋為奮武將軍以助焉。

translations.literal: When the memorial was presented, the Son of Heaven on a large scale raised more than sixty thousand troops and appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies Marquis of Yiyang Ren Qianqiu as general Who Rouses Might to assist.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor exploded sixty thousand reinforcements onto the road and named Grand Master Ren Qianqiu of Yiyang general Who Rouses Might to second him.

sentences.zh: 奉世上言：「願得其眾，不須煩大將。」

translations.literal: Fengshi submitted upward, saying, "Your servant begs to obtain those troops and has no need to trouble a great general."
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi wrote back, "Give me those men; no need for another grand commander."

sentences.zh: 因陳轉輸之費。

translations.literal: Thereupon he set forth the costs of transport.
translations.idiomatic: He itemized the cost of supply.

sentences.zh: 上於是以璽書勞奉世，且讓之，曰：「皇帝問將兵右將軍，甚苦暴露。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon with a rescript on the imperial seal consoled Fengshi and also reproached him, saying, "The emperor asks the right general who leads troops: you have greatly suffered exposure in the open.
translations.idiomatic: The throne answered with a sealed rescript both praising and scolding: "The emperor asks his right general: you have borne hard weather long.

sentences.zh: 羌虜侵邊境，殺吏民，甚逆天道，故遣將軍帥士大夫行天誅。

translations.literal: The Qiang raiders encroach on the border, kill officials and common people, greatly oppose Heaven's way; therefore We dispatch the general to lead gentlemen and officials to carry out Heaven's punishment.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang murder our people and offend Heaven; We send you to execute that sentence with gentry and troops.

sentences.zh: 以將軍材質之美，奮精兵，誅不軌，百下百全之道也。

translations.literal: With the general's fine quality of stuff and wielding crack troops to execute the lawless, it is the way of a hundred descents and a hundred completenesses.
translations.idiomatic: Given your gifts and picked soldiers, lawless men should fall at the first blow.

sentences.zh: 今乃有畔敵之名，大為中國羞。

translations.literal: Now instead there is the name of a rival enemy, greatly shaming the Middle Kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: Yet rumor speaks of stalemate—a deep shame to China.

sentences.zh: 以昔不閑習之故邪？

translations.literal: Is it because of former lack of practice and drill?
translations.idiomatic: Is it want of training?

sentences.zh: 以恩厚未洽，信約不明也？

translations.literal: Or because favor and kindness have not fully penetrated, trust and covenant are not clear?
translations.idiomatic: Or because our kindness and clear pledges have not reached them?

sentences.zh: 朕甚怪之。

translations.literal: We are greatly puzzled by it.
translations.idiomatic: We are much perplexed.

sentences.zh: 上書言羌虜依深山，多徑道，不得不多分部遮要害，須得後發營士，足以決事，部署已定，勢不可復置大將，聞之。

translations.literal: Your memorial states that the Qiang raiders rely on deep mountains and many bypaths, so you cannot but divide the army in many parts to block the critical points, and that you must await later-arriving camp troops to be sufficient to settle the affair; deployment is already fixed and the situation cannot again accommodate a great general—We have heard it.
translations.idiomatic: Your letter says the Qiang hold the heights and many trails, so you must split the army to seal choke points and wait for reinforcements to decide the fight; dispositions are set and a new high commander would only tangle them—We have read you.

sentences.zh: 前為將軍兵少，不足自守，故發近所騎，日夜詣，非為擊也。

translations.literal: Earlier, because the general's troops were few and insufficient to guard yourselves, therefore We raised nearby cavalry, day and night hurrying to you—it was not for attack.
translations.idiomatic: We rushed local cavalry to you earlier because your numbers could not hold the line, not to second-guess your tactics.

sentences.zh: 今發三輔、河東、弘農越騎、跡射、佽飛、彀者、羽林孤兒及呼速累、嗕種，方急遣。

translations.literal: Now We are dispatching in urgent haste the swift riders, trace-and-shoot archers, flying spearmen, crossbowmen, Forest of Feathers orphans, and the Husuli and Ru tribes from the three adjuncts, Hedong, and Hongnang.
translations.idiomatic: We now press toward you the swift riders, trace archers, flying spears, crossbow specialists, palace orphans, and tribal auxiliaries from the capital districts, Hedong, and Hongnang.

sentences.zh: 且兵，凶器也，必有成敗者，患策不豫定，料敵不審也，故復遣奮武將軍。

translations.literal: Moreover troops are inauspicious instruments; there must be those who succeed or fail—the worry is that plans are not settled in advance and the enemy is not carefully gauged; therefore We again dispatch the general Who Rouses Might.
translations.idiomatic: Arms are ill omens; victory turns on forethought and intelligence of the foe—hence the second general Who Rouses Might.

sentences.zh: 兵法曰大將軍出必有偏裨，所以揚威武，參計策，將軍又何疑焉？

translations.literal: Military texts say that when a great general goes out there must be wing commanders, whereby martial awesomeness is displayed and plans are consulted—why should the general doubt?
translations.idiomatic: The canon says every commander needs lieutenants to lend weight and counsel—do not take offense.

sentences.zh: 夫愛吏士，得眾心，舉而無悔，禽敵必全，將軍之職也。

translations.literal: To cherish officers and soldiers, win the host's hearts, move without regret, and capture the enemy completely—this is a general's duty.
translations.idiomatic: Cherish the men, win their loyalty, strike without regret, and annihilate the foe—that is your charge.

sentences.zh: 若乃轉輸之費，則有司存，將軍勿憂。

translations.literal: As for the costs of transport, the offices exist for that; let the general not worry.
translations.idiomatic: Supply is the ministries' problem, not yours.

sentences.zh: 須奮武將軍兵到，合擊羌虜。」

translations.literal: Await the arrival of the troops of the general Who Rouses Might and jointly strike the Qiang raiders."
translations.idiomatic: Stand until Who Rouses Might arrives, then crush the Qiang together."

sentences.zh: 十月，兵畢至隴西。

translations.literal: In the tenth month the troops had all reached Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: By the tenth month every column had reached Longxi.

sentences.zh: 十一月，並進。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month they advanced together.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month they attacked as one.

sentences.zh: 羌虜大破，斬首數千級，餘皆走出塞。

translations.literal: The Qiang raiders were greatly broken; several thousand heads were cut off, and the rest all fled out beyond the passes.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang broke; thousands fell, the rest bolted beyond the wall.

sentences.zh: 兵未決間，漢復發募士萬人，拜定襄太守韓安國為建威將軍。

translations.literal: While the issue was not yet decided, Han again issued a call for ten thousand volunteers and appointed governor of Dingxiang Han Anguo as general Who Establishes Might.
translations.idiomatic: Mid-campaign Han raised another ten thousand recruits and named Dingxiang governor Han Anguo general Who Establishes Might.

sentences.zh: 未進，聞羌破，還。

translations.literal: Before he advanced, hearing that the Qiang were broken, he returned.
translations.idiomatic: Han Anguo turned back when he heard the Qiang had collapsed.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「羌虜破散創艾，亡逃出塞，其罷吏士，頗留屯田，備要害處。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "The Qiang raiders are broken and scattered, wounded and in flight, escaping beyond the passes; dismiss the officers and soldiers, but rather leave many to garrison fields and guard critical points."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "The Qiang are shattered; demobilize the host but leave enough men to farm the garrisons and hold the choke points."

sentences.zh: 明年二月，奉世還京師，更為左將軍光祿勳如故。

translations.literal: In the second month of the following year Fengshi returned to the capital and was again made left general and superintendent of the household as before.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, second month, he reached the capital, restored as left general and superintendent of the household.

sentences.zh: 其後錄功拜爵，下詔曰：「羌虜桀黠，賊害吏民，攻隴西府寺，燔燒置亭，絕道橋，甚逆天道。

translations.literal: Afterward his merit was recorded and noble rank granted; an edict said, "The Qiang raiders are cruel and cunning, injuring officials and common people, attacking Longxi offices, burning relay stations, cutting roads and bridges, greatly opposing Heaven's way.
translations.idiomatic: Merit rolls later brought an edict: "The Qiang are savage foes who slew our people, torched Longxi yamen and post houses, and cut bridges—Heaven's enemies.

sentences.zh: 左將軍光祿勳奉世前將兵征討，斬捕首虜八千餘級，鹵馬、牛、羊以萬數。

translations.literal: Left general and superintendent of the household Fengshi formerly led troops in campaign and strike, beheading and capturing more than eight thousand heads, and seizing horses, oxen, and sheep by the ten thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Left General Fengshi took the field, took over eight thousand heads, and drove off countless stock.

sentences.zh: 賜奉世爵關內侯，良邑五百戶，黃金六十斤。」

translations.literal: Let Fengshi be granted rank of marquis within the passes, good fief of five hundred households, and sixty jin of gold."
translations.idiomatic: We grant him marquis-within-the-passes rank, five hundred good households, and sixty jin of gold."

sentences.zh: 裨將、校尉三十餘人，皆拜。

translations.literal: More than thirty lieutenant generals and colonels were all ennobled.
translations.idiomatic: Over thirty subordinate generals and colonels received titles with him.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，奉世病卒。

translations.literal: After a little more than a year Fengshi died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: A year or so later he died of illness.

sentences.zh: 居爪牙官前後十年，為折衝宿將，功名次趙充國。

translations.literal: He held fang-and-claw offices for ten years running, a veteran general who broke enemy assaults; in order of merit he ranked next to Zhao Chongguo.
translations.idiomatic: For a full decade he stood in the emperor's mailed fist—an old hand at turning assaults—second in renown only to Zhao Chongguo.

sentences.zh: 奮武將軍任千秋者，其父宮，昭帝時以丞相征事捕斬反者左將軍上官桀，封侯，宣帝時為太常，薨。

translations.literal: General Who Rouses Might Ren Qianqiu—his father was Ren Gong, who in Emperor Zhao's time, as investigating clerk to the chancellor, captured and beheaded the rebel left general Shangguan Jie, was ennobled marquis, became grand master of ceremonies under Emperor Xuan, and died.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Qianqiu's father Ren Gong had served Zhao's chancellor as investigator, cut down mutineer Shangguan Jie, taken a marquisate, and ended as grand master of ceremonies under Xuan.

sentences.zh: 千秋嗣後，復為太常。

translations.literal: Qianqiu inherited afterward and again became grand master of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: Qianqiu inherited the fief and likewise rose to grand master of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 成帝時，樂昌侯王商代奉世為左將軍，而千秋為右將軍，後亦為左將軍。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng's time Marquis of Lechang Wang Shang replaced Fengshi as left general, while Qianqiu was right general; later he too became left general.
translations.idiomatic: Under Cheng, Wang Shang of Lechang succeeded Fengshi as left general while Qianqiu held the right, then moved left in turn.

sentences.zh: 子孫傳國，至王莽乃絕云。

translations.literal: Sons and grandsons transmitted the state until Wang Mang, when it was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The line held the marquisate until Wang Mang ended it.

sentences.zh: 奉世死後二年，西域都護甘延壽以誅郅支單于封為列侯。

translations.literal: Two years after Fengshi's death, protector-general of the Western Regions Gan Yanshou was ennobled full marquis for executing Chanyu Zhizhi.
translations.idiomatic: Two years after Fengshi died, Gan Yanshou won a full marquisate for killing Zhizhi.

sentences.zh: 時，丞相匡衡亦用延壽矯制生事，據蕭望之前議，以為不當封，而議者咸美其功，上從眾而侯之。

translations.literal: At the time Chancellor Kuang Heng also cited Yanshou's forging an edict and creating trouble, relying on Xiao Wangzhi's earlier argument, and held that he should not be enfeoffed, but debaters all praised his merit, and the emperor followed the multitude and made him marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Kuang Heng echoed Wangzhi's old line that Yanshou had overstepped, yet public praise swayed the throne and Yanshou was ennobled.

sentences.zh: 於是杜欽上疏，追訟奉世前功曰：「前莎車王殺漢使者，約諸國背畔。

translations.literal: Thereupon Du Qin submitted a memorial, retrospectively pleading Fengshi's earlier merit, saying, "Formerly the king of Shache killed Han's envoy and bound the states in rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Du Qin then memorialized to reopen Fengshi's case: Shache had murdered an envoy and rallied the oases against Han.

sentences.zh: 左將軍奉世以衛候便宜發兵誅莎車王，策定城郭，功施邊境。

translations.literal: Left general Fengshi, as guard commandant, on convenient authority raised troops and executed the king of Shache, settled strategy for the walled towns, and his merit reached the border.
translations.idiomatic: Left General Fengshi, still a guard commandant, had seized initiative, beheaded the Shache king, and secured every oasis.

sentences.zh: 議者以奉世奉使有指，《春秋》之義亡遂事，漢家之法有矯制，故不得侯。

translations.literal: Debaters held that Fengshi had gone on mission with definite orders, that by the meaning of the Spring and Autumn Annals one does not pursue completed deeds, and that Han law has the article on forging edicts; therefore he could not be made marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Critics said he had fixed orders, the Annals forbade second-guessing a finished act, and Han law punished forged rescripts—hence no fief.

sentences.zh: 令匈奴郅支單于殺漢使者，亡保康居，都護延壽發城郭兵屯田吏士四萬餘人以誅斬之，封為列侯。

translations.literal: Now the Xiongnu Chanyu Zhizhi has killed Han's envoy, fled for refuge to Kangju, and the protector-general Yanshou raised more than forty thousand men of the walled towns and garrison-field officers and soldiers to execute and behead him, and he is enfeoffed full marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Zhizhi murdered an envoy and fled to Kangju; Yanshou led forty thousand colonists and garrison troops to kill him—and took a full marquisate.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為比罪則郅支薄，量敵則莎車眾，用師則奉世寡，計勝則奉世為功於邊境安，慮敗則延壽為禍於國家深。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly holds that comparing crimes Zhizhi's is lighter, measuring enemies Shache's host was larger, using troops Fengshi's were fewer; reckoning victory Fengshi's merit was in securing the border, considering defeat Yanshou's harm to the state was deeper.
translations.idiomatic: Zhizhi's guilt was slighter, Shache's coalition larger, Fengshi's army smaller; success made Fengshi the border's shield, failure would have made Yanshou a heavier national disaster.

sentences.zh: 其違命而擅生事同，延壽割地封，而奉世獨不錄。

translations.literal: In disobeying orders and on their own creating trouble they are the same; Yanshou was granted land enfeoffment while Fengshi alone is not recorded.
translations.idiomatic: Both broke orders to act; one was carved a fief, the other erased from the rolls.

sentences.zh: 臣聞功同賞異則勞臣疑，罪鈞刑殊則百姓惑；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that when merit is the same but rewards differ, laboring ministers doubt; when guilt is even but punishments differ, the hundred surnames are perplexed;
translations.idiomatic: Equal deeds and unequal rewards breed suspicion in loyal ministers; equal crimes and unequal sentences confuse the people;

sentences.zh: 疑生無常，惑生不知所從；

translations.literal: from doubt arises lack of constancy, from perplexity arises not knowing which way to follow;
translations.idiomatic: doubt unmoors duty, confusion leaves them nowhere to turn;

sentences.zh: 亡常則節趨不立，不知所從則百姓無所措手足。

translations.literal: lack of constancy means integrity and tendency do not stand; not knowing which way to follow means the hundred surnames have nowhere to put hand or foot.
translations.idiomatic: without a fixed standard virtue cannot root, and the common folk flounder.

sentences.zh: 奉世圖難忘死，信命殊俗，威功白著，為世使表，獨抑厭而不揚，非聖主所以塞疑厲節之意也。

translations.literal: Fengshi planned through hardship and forgot death, trusted his fate among alien customs; his awesomeness and merit were plain for the world, a model for envoys—yet alone he is suppressed and not celebrated; this is not the meaning by which a sage ruler blocks doubt and sharpens integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi risked death in barbarian lands and his glory shone—an exemplar for every envoy—yet the court buries him; that is no way for a sage king to clear doubt and steel character.

sentences.zh: 願下有司議。」

translations.literal: I beg that it be sent down to the offices for deliberation."
translations.idiomatic: I ask the ministries to reconsider."

sentences.zh: 上以先帝時事，不復錄。

translations.literal: The emperor, because it was a matter of the late emperor's time, did not again record it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor let the old case lie as a prior reign's affair.

sentences.zh: 奉世有子男九人，女四人。

translations.literal: Fengshi had nine sons and four daughters.
translations.idiomatic: Fengshi fathered nine sons and four daughters.

sentences.zh: 長女媛以選充兵宮，為元帝昭儀，產中山孝王。

translations.literal: The eldest daughter Yuan was selected to enter the rear palace, became a Brilliant Companion to Emperor Yuan, and bore the filial king of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest daughter Yuan entered the harem as Brilliant Companion to Yuan and bore the king of Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 元帝崩，媛為中山太后，隨王就國。

translations.literal: When Emperor Yuan died, Yuan became grand empress dowager of Zhongshan and followed the king to his state.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Yuan died she became dowager of Zhongshan and went with her son to his fief.

sentences.zh: 奉世長子譚，太常舉孝廉為郎，功次補天水司馬。

translations.literal: Fengshi's eldest son Tan was recommended filial and incorrupt by the grand master of ceremonies and became a gentleman, then by order of merit was appointed marshal of Tianshui.
translations.idiomatic: Eldest son Tan, filial and incorrupt from the grand master of ceremonies' nomination, became a gentleman and then Tianshui marshal by seniority.

sentences.zh: 奉世擊西羌，譚為校尉，隨父從軍有功，未拜病死。

translations.literal: When Fengshi struck the Western Qiang, Tan was a colonel; following his father in the army he had merit, but before appointment he died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Tan served as colonel in the Qiang war and earned merit beside his father, then died of sickness before promotion.

sentences.zh: 譚弟野王、逡、立、參至大官。

translations.literal: Tan's younger brothers Yewang, Qun, Li, and Shen reached high office.
translations.idiomatic: Younger brothers Yewang, Qun, Li, and Shen all rose high.

sentences.zh: 野王字君卿，受業博士，通《詩》。

translations.literal: Yewang, courtesy name Junqing, received instruction from an erudite and was conversant with the Odes.
translations.idiomatic: Yewang, style Junqing, studied under an erudite and mastered the Odes.

sentences.zh: 少以父任為太子中庶子。

translations.literal: In youth by his father's privilege he became attendant to the heir apparent for the inner court.
translations.idiomatic: As a youth he entered the heir apparent's household by yin privilege.

sentences.zh: 年十八，上書願試守長安令。

translations.literal: At age eighteen he submitted a memorial begging to be tried as acting magistrate of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: At eighteen he volunteered to trial-run the magistracy of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 宣帝奇其志，問丞相魏相，相以為不可許。

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan marveled at his ambition and asked Chancellor Wei Xiang, who held it could not be permitted.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan admired the audacity; Chancellor Wei Xiang said no.

sentences.zh: 後以功次補當陽長，遷為櫟陽令，徙夏陽令。

translations.literal: Later by order of merit he was appointed magistrate of Dangyang, was promoted magistrate of Liyang, and transferred magistrate of Xiayang.
translations.idiomatic: He later rose through Dangyang, Liyang, and Xiayang by merit.

sentences.zh: 元帝時，遷隴西太守，以治行高，入為左馮翊。

translations.literal: In Emperor Yuan's time he was promoted governor of Longxi; because his administration was high in quality, he entered as left governor of Fengyi.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yuan he governed Longxi with such distinction that the capital made him left governor of Fengyi.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，而池陽令並素行貪污，輕野王外戚年少，治行不改。

translations.literal: After more than a year, the magistrate of Chiyang, Bing, by nature corrupt in conduct, despised Yewang as a young maternal relative of the consort and did not reform his administration.
translations.idiomatic: A year in, Chiyang magistrate Bing, a grafter, sneered at Yewang as a young in-law and kept stealing.

sentences.zh: 野王部督郵掾示殳祤趙都案驗，得其主守盜十金罪，收捕。

translations.literal: Yewang ordered his regional inspector clerk to display the case to Zhao Du of Shuyi for investigation; obtaining proof of Bing's crime of guarding office property and stealing ten jin of gold, he arrested him.
translations.idiomatic: Yewang set his inspector on Bing; Zhao Du of Shuyi proved ten jin of embezzled gold and moved to arrest him.

sentences.zh: 並不首吏，都格殺。

translations.literal: Bing would not submit to the clerks; Du struck and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Bing resisted arrest and Du killed him in the scuffle.

sentences.zh: 並家上書陳冤，事下廷尉。

translations.literal: Bing's family submitted a memorial stating grievance; the matter went down to the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: Bing's kin appealed; the file went to the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 都詣吏自殺以明野王，京師稱其威信，遷為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Du went to the clerks and committed suicide to clear Yewang; the capital praised his trustworthiness and awe, and Yewang was promoted grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: Du killed himself at the yamen gate to spare Yewang; the capital praised his loyalty and promoted Yewang to grand herald.

sentences.zh: 數年，御史大夫李延壽病卒，在位多舉野王。

translations.literal: Several years later Imperial Counselor Li Yanshou died of illness; many in office recommended Yewang.
translations.idiomatic: When Counselor Li Yanshou died, the bureaucracy backed Yewang.

sentences.zh: 上使尚書選第中二千石，而野王行能第一。

translations.literal: The emperor had the secretariat rank the full two-thousand-bushel officials; Yewang's conduct and ability were first.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered the secretariat to rank the two-thousand-bushel corps; Yewang topped the list.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾用野王為三公，後世必謂我私後宮親屬，以野王為比。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "If We employ Yewang among the three dukes, later generations will surely say We favored inner-palace kin, taking Yewang as the precedent."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "If I name him one of the Three Dukes, posterity will say I bought favor for the harem."

sentences.zh: 乃下詔曰：「剛強堅固，確然亡欲，大鴻臚野王是也。

translations.literal: Thereupon he issued an edict, saying, "Rigid and firm, steadfastly without desire—Grand Herald Yewang is such.
translations.idiomatic: So he issued a litany of virtues: "Grand Herald Yewang is granite and free of appetite;

sentences.zh: 心辨善辭，可使四方，少府五鹿充宗是也。

translations.literal: Mind discerning and skilled in speech, able to be sent to the four quarters—Junior Privy Treasurer Wulu Chongzong is such.
translations.idiomatic: Wulu Chongzong has a ready tongue for embassies;

sentences.zh: 廉潔節儉，太子少傅張譚是也。

translations.literal: Incorrupt and frugal—Junior tutor to the heir apparent Zhang Tan is such.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tan is clean and spare;

sentences.zh: 其以少傅為御史大夫。」

translations.literal: Let the junior tutor become imperial counselor."
translations.idiomatic: let the junior tutor take the counselor's seal."

sentences.zh: 上繇下第而用譚，越次避嫌不用野王，以昭儀兄故也。

translations.literal: The emperor for this reason used Tan, who had ranked low, skipping order to avoid suspicion and not employing Yewang, because he was the elder brother of the Brilliant Companion.
translations.idiomatic: He picked lower-ranked Zhang Tan to dodge the charge of nepotism—Yewang was the Brilliant Companion's brother.

sentences.zh: 野王乃歎曰：「人皆以女寵貴，我兄弟獨以賤！」

translations.literal: Yewang thereupon sighed, saying, "Others all become noble through a woman's favor; we brothers alone are brought low by it!"
translations.idiomatic: Yewang groaned, "Other men ride their sisters' skirts upward; our sister's skirt drags us down."

sentences.zh: 野王雖不為三公，甚見器重，有名當世。

translations.literal: Although Yewang did not become one of the three dukes, he was greatly valued and esteemed and had a name in his generation.
translations.idiomatic: He never took a ducal chair yet remained indispensable and famous.

sentences.zh: 成帝立，有司奏野王王舅，不宜備九卿，以秩出為上郡太守，加賜黃金百斤。

translations.literal: When Emperor Cheng was established, the offices reported that Yewang, as the king's maternal uncle, was not fit to fill one of the nine ministers; by rank he was sent out as governor of Shang commandery, with an added gift of one hundred jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: At Cheng's accession the ministries barred maternal uncles from the nine ministers; Yewang went to Shang commandery with a hundred jin of gold as consolation.

sentences.zh: 朔方刺史蕭育奏封事，薦言：「野王行能高妙，內足與圖身，外足以慮化。

translations.literal: The regional inspector of Shuofang, Xiao Yu, submitted a sealed memorial recommending, saying, "Yewang's conduct and ability are lofty and subtle; inwardly sufficient to plan for the self, outwardly sufficient to consider transformation.
translations.idiomatic: Shuofang inspector Xiao Yu sealed a brief praising Yewang's inner discipline and outer statesmanship;

sentences.zh: 竊惜野王懷國之寶，而不得陪朝廷與朝者並。

translations.literal: Your servant privately pities Yewang for cherishing the state's treasure yet not being able to stand in court with those who attend audience.
translations.idiomatic: he mourned that such a jewel stood outside the hall;

sentences.zh: 野王前以王舅出，以賢復入，明國家樂進賢也。」

translations.literal: Yewang earlier went out because he was the king's maternal uncle; now to re-enter on account of worth shows that the state delights in advancing the worthy."
translations.idiomatic: to recall him for talent would prove the court loves worth over kin."

sentences.zh: 上自為太子時聞知野王。

translations.literal: The emperor had known of Yewang since he himself was heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng had admired Yewang since his own princedom.

sentences.zh: 會其病免，復以故二千石使行河堤，因拜為琅邪太守。

translations.literal: It happened that he was dismissed on illness; again, as a former two-thousand-bushel official, he was sent to inspect the river dikes, and thereupon was appointed governor of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: When illness cashiered him, the court sent him, still at two-thousand-bushel rank, to inspect dikes, then named him governor of Langye.

sentences.zh: 是時，成帝長舅陽平侯王鳳為大司馬大將軍，輔政八九年矣，時數有災異，京兆尹王章譏鳳專權不可任用，薦野王代鳳。

translations.literal: At that time the emperor's senior maternal uncle, Marquis of Yangping Wang Feng, was grand marshal and great general, aiding the government eight or nine years; at the time there were frequent disasters and prodigies; the governor of the capital, Wang Zhang, ridiculed Feng as wielding sole power unfit for employment, and recommended Yewang to replace Feng.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng, Cheng's senior uncle, had run the state nearly a decade when capital governor Wang Zhang attacked his monopoly and nominated Yewang to replace him.

sentences.zh: 上初納其言，而後誅章，語在《元后傳》。

translations.literal: The emperor at first accepted his words, but afterward executed Zhang—the account is in the "Biography of the Yuan Empress Dowager."
translations.idiomatic: The throne first agreed, then executed Wang Zhang—the tale is told with the Yuan empress.

sentences.zh: 於是野王懼不自安，遂病，滿三月賜告，與妻子歸杜陵就醫藥。

translations.literal: Thereupon Yewang was afraid and not at ease in himself; he fell ill, and when three months of sick leave were granted, he went with wife and children to Duling to seek medicine.
translations.idiomatic: Yewang grew terrified, took three months' medical leave, and fled to Duling with his family.

sentences.zh: 大將軍鳳風御史中丞劾奏野王賜告養病而私自便，持虎符出界歸家，奉詔不敬。

translations.literal: Great general Feng hinted to the palace secretary to memorialize impeachment: Yewang had received granted sick leave to nurse illness but privately took convenience, held the tiger tally and crossed the boundary to return home—disrespect to an edict.
translations.idiomatic: Feng nudged the palace secretary to charge that Yewang had abused sick leave, crossed out of his command with the tiger tally, and insulted imperial grace.

sentences.zh: 杜欽時在大將軍莫府，欽素高野王父子行能，奏記於鳳，為野王言曰：「竊見令曰，吏二千石告，過長安謁，不分別予賜。

translations.literal: Du Qin was then in the great general's headquarters; Qin had always esteemed the conduct and ability of Yewang and his father, and submitted a note to Feng, speaking for Yewang, saying, "Your servant has seen in the ordinance that when officials of two thousand bushels take leave and pass through Chang'an to audience, it does not distinguish granting ordinary versus granted sick leave.
translations.idiomatic: Du Qin, in Feng's staff, argued the statute: two-thousand-bushel men passing through the capital on leave were not split between "ordinary" and "granted" sick leave;

sentences.zh: 今有司以為予告得歸，賜告不得，是一律兩科，失省刑之意。

translations.literal: Now the offices hold that ordinary leave may return home but granted sick leave may not—this is one statute with two grades and loses the meaning of reviewing punishments.
translations.idiomatic: treating the two differently was one law with two faces and betrayed the spirit of merciful judgment;

sentences.zh: 夫三最予告，令也；

translations.literal: The three highest ratings granting ordinary leave is ordinance;
translations.idiomatic: top-rated merit leave was statute;

sentences.zh: 病滿三月賜告，詔恩也。

translations.literal: sick leave of three months granted is edict grace.
translations.idiomatic: three months' imperial sick leave was grace;

sentences.zh: 令告則得，詔恩不得，失輕重之差。

translations.literal: Ordinance allows it but edict grace does not—this misses the balance of light and heavy.
translations.idiomatic: to honor one and forbid the other skewed weight and weightlessness;

sentences.zh: 又二千石病賜告得歸有故事，不得去郡亡著令。

translations.literal: Moreover for two-thousand-bushel illness with granted leave to return home there is precedent; there is no written ordinance forbidding leaving the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: precedent let sick two-thousand-bushel men go home, and no written rule barred leaving the command.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『賞疑從予，所以廣恩勸功也；

translations.literal: Tradition says, "When reward is doubtful, incline to grant—thereby broadening grace and encouraging merit;
translations.idiomatic: The tradition runs, "If reward is doubtful, give—thus widening grace;

sentences.zh: 罰疑從去，所以慎刑，闕難知也。』

translations.literal: when punishment is doubtful, incline to remit—thereby being careful with punishments and leaving what is hard to know."
translations.idiomatic: if punishment is doubtful, withhold—thus sparing the blade for gray cases."

sentences.zh: 今釋令與故事而假不敬之法，甚違闕疑從去之意。

translations.literal: Now to discard ordinance and precedent and borrow the law of irreverence greatly violates the meaning of "when punishment is doubtful, remit."
translations.idiomatic: To scrap precedent for a vague "disrespect" charge betrayed that maxim.

sentences.zh: 即以二千石守千里之地，任兵馬之重，不宜去郡，將以制刑為後法者，則野王之罪，在未制令前也。

translations.literal: Even if, because a two-thousand-bushel guards a thousand li of territory and bears the weight of arms and horses, he ought not leave the commandery, intending to make punishment regulation for later law—then Yewang's offense lies before the ordinance was made.
translations.idiomatic: Even if a new rule should chain governors to their posts, Yewang acted before any such rule existed.

sentences.zh: 刑賞大信，不可不慎。」

translations.literal: Punishment and reward are great trust—they cannot but be careful."
translations.idiomatic: Reward and punishment are the throne's bond with the realm—handle them with care."

sentences.zh: 鳳不聽，竟免野王。

translations.literal: Feng would not listen and in the end dismissed Yewang.
translations.idiomatic: Feng ignored him and cashiered Yewang anyway.

sentences.zh: 郡國二千石病賜告不得歸家，自此始。

translations.literal: That commandery and kingdom two-thousand-bushels on granted sick leave could not return home began from this.
translations.idiomatic: From that day governors on sick leave could not go home.

sentences.zh: 初，野王嗣父爵為關內侯，免歸。

translations.literal: Earlier, Yewang had inherited his father's rank as marquis within the passes and been dismissed to return home.
translations.idiomatic: Yewang had earlier inherited marquis-within-the-passes rank and lost it.

sentences.zh: 數年，年老，終於家。

translations.literal: Several years later, old in years, he ended at home.
translations.idiomatic: Years later he died at home in old age.

sentences.zh: 子座嗣爵，至孫坐中山太后事絕。

translations.literal: His son Zuo inherited the rank; reaching the grandson, it was cut off on account of the affair of the grand empress dowager of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Son Zuo inherited until a grandson lost the title in the Zhongshan dowager scandal.

sentences.zh: 逡字子產，通《易》，太常察孝廉為郎，補謁者。

translations.literal: Qun, courtesy name Zichan, was conversant with the Changes; the grand master of ceremonies inspected him as filial and incorrupt and he became a gentleman, supplemented yeoman.
translations.idiomatic: Qun, style Zichan, knew the Changes, entered as filial and incorrupt, and became a yeoman.

sentences.zh: 建昭中，選為復土校尉。

translations.literal: In the Jianzhao period he was selected colonel of the burial mounds.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianzhao he was picked as colonel of the burial mounds.

sentences.zh: 光祿勳於永舉茂材，為美陽令。

translations.literal: Superintendent of the household Yu Yong recommended him as flourishing talent, and he became magistrate of Meiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Yong nominated him flourishing talent for Meiyang.

sentences.zh: 功次遷長樂屯衛司馬，清河都尉，隴西太守。

translations.literal: By order of merit he was promoted garrison marshal of Changle, chief commandant of Qinghe, and governor of Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Merit raised him to Changle garrison marshal, Qinghe chief commandant, and Longxi governor.

sentences.zh: 治行廉平，年四十餘卒。

translations.literal: His administration was incorrupt and even; he died past forty.
translations.idiomatic: He governed cleanly and died in his forties.

sentences.zh: 為都尉時，言河堤方略，在《溝洫志》。

translations.literal: When he was chief commandant he spoke on river-dike strategy; it is in the "Treatise on Ditches and Channels."
translations.idiomatic: As chief commandant he wrote on flood control—see the "Treatise on Ditches and Channels."

sentences.zh: 立字聖卿，通《春秋》。

translations.literal: Li, courtesy name Shengqing, was conversant with the Spring and Autumn Annals.
translations.idiomatic: Li, style Shengqing, mastered the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 以父任為郎，稍遷諸曹。

translations.literal: By his father's privilege he became a gentleman and gradually was promoted among the various bureaus.
translations.idiomatic: Yin privilege made him a gentleman; he climbed the bureau ladder.

sentences.zh: 竟寧中，以王舅出為五原屬國都尉。

translations.literal: In the Jingning period, because he was the king's maternal uncle, he went out as chief commandant of the dependent state of Wuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Jingning sent him, as the prince's uncle, to command Wuyuan dependent state.

sentences.zh: 數年，遷五原太守，徙西河、上郡。

translations.literal: After several years he was promoted governor of Wuyuan, then transferred to Xihe and Shang commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to Wuyuan governor, then Xihe and Shang.

sentences.zh: 立居職公廉，治行略與野王相似，而多知有恩貸，好為條教。

translations.literal: Li in office was fair and incorrupt; his administration was broadly similar to Yewang's, but he knew more and showed kindness in loans, liking to issue detailed instructions.
translations.idiomatic: Like Yewang he was just, but kinder with forbearance and fonder of written instructions.

sentences.zh: 吏民嘉美野王、立相代為太守，歌之曰：「大馮君，小馮君，兄弟繼踵相因循，聰明賢知惠吏民，政如魯、衛德化鈞，周公、康叔猶二君。」

translations.literal: Officials and commoners praised Yewang and Li succeeding each other as governors, singing, "Great lord Feng, little lord Feng, brothers following heel to heel in succession, wise and worthy, kind to officials and common people; government like Lu and Wei, transformation even; the dukes of Zhou and Kangshu—still two lords."
translations.idiomatic: The people sang when the brothers swapped seats: "Big Feng, little Feng, back to back the brothers go, clever and kind to clerk and farmer, their rule like Lu and Wei in tune—two dukes of Zhou for us."

sentences.zh: 後遷為東海太守，下濕病痺。

translations.literal: Later he was promoted governor of Donghai; the low dampness brought arthritic paralysis.
translations.idiomatic: Promotion to Donghai left him crippled by damp.

sentences.zh: 天子聞之，徙立為太原太守。

translations.literal: When the Son of Heaven heard it, he transferred Li to be governor of Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor moved him to the dry climate of Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 更歷五郡，所居有跡。

translations.literal: He passed through five commanderies in succession; wherever he dwelt he left a record.
translations.idiomatic: Five commands in a row felt his mark.

sentences.zh: 年老卒官。

translations.literal: Old in years, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in harness, full of years.

sentences.zh: 頃之，哀帝即位，帝祖母傅太后用事，追怨參姊中山太后，陷以祝詛大逆之罪，語在《外戚傳》。

translations.literal: Before long Emperor Ai ascended the throne; the emperor's grandmother Grand Empress Dowager Fu held power, nursed resentment against Shen's elder sister the grand empress dowager of Zhongshan, and trapped her with the great irreverence charge of curse-praying—the account is in the "Biography of the Outer Kin."
translations.idiomatic: Soon Ai ruled; Grand Empress Dowager Fu hated Shen's sister, the Zhongshan dowager, and framed her for witchcraft—see the outer-kin annals.

sentences.zh: 參以同產當相坐，謁者承製召參詣廷尉，參自殺。

translations.literal: Shen, as born of the same womb, was to suffer joint liability; the yeoman, carrying the edict, summoned Shen to the commandant of justice, and Shen killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Shen faced kin liability; the usher's summons to the commandant of justice drove him to suicide.

sentences.zh: 且死，仰天歎曰：「參父子兄弟皆備大位，身至封侯，今被惡名而死，姊弟不敢自惜，傷無以見先人於地下！」

translations.literal: About to die, he sighed toward heaven, saying, "Shen's fathers, sons, and brothers have all filled high posts, bodies reaching enfeoffed marquis; now bearing a foul name I die; as for sister and brother I dare not pity myself—I grieve that I have no way to see the ancestors underground!"
translations.idiomatic: Facing death he cried to Heaven: "Our house filled every high seat and took marquisates—now I die disgraced; I do not pity myself or my sister, only that I cannot face our fathers below the sod."

sentences.zh: 死者十七人，眾莫不憐之。

translations.literal: Seventeen died; none among the multitude did not pity them.
translations.idiomatic: Seventeen lives ended; the realm wept for them.

sentences.zh: 宗族徙歸故郡。

translations.literal: The lineage was moved back to the old commandery.
translations.idiomatic: The clan was deported to its ancestral commandery.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：《詩》稱「抑抑威儀，惟德之隅。」

translations.literal: The appraising comment says: The Odes praises "solemn, solemn awe-inspiring deportment—the corner of virtue."
translations.idiomatic: The historian cites the Odes: "Grave is the bearing that fences virtue."

sentences.zh: 宜鄉侯參鞠躬履方，擇地而行，可謂淑人君子，然卒死於非罪，不能自免，哀哉！

translations.literal: Marquis of Yixiang Shen bowed the body and trod the square, choosing the ground in walking—he may be called a virtuous man and noble man, yet in the end he died of a crime he did not commit, unable to save himself—alas!
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Shen walked every inch by the book—a true gentleman—yet perished on a false charge he could not escape.

sentences.zh: 讒邪交亂，貞良被害，自古而然。

translations.literal: Slander and evil crisscrossing, the chaste and good injured—this has been so since antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Slander devouring the good is as old as history.

sentences.zh: 故伯奇放流，孟子宮刑，申生雉經，屈原赴湘，《小弁》之詩作，《離騷》之辭興。

translations.literal: Therefore Boyi was banished, Mengzi the Lu eunuch suffered castration, Sheng'er strangled with pheasant cord, Qu Yuan threw himself into the Xiang; the poem "Little Boy" was composed, the words of "Li sao" arose.
translations.idiomatic: So Boyi was driven out, Mengzi the palace attendant was castrated, Crown Prince Sheng hanged himself, Qu Yuan drowned—the "Little Boy" ode and "Li sao" rose from such pain.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「心之憂矣，涕既隕之。」

translations.literal: The classic says, "The heart's sorrow—tears already fall."
translations.idiomatic: The canon says, "The heart's grief already wets the cheek with tears."

sentences.zh: 馮參姊弟，亦云悲矣！

translations.literal: Shen's elder sister and younger brother—one may also say it was pitiful!
translations.idiomatic: Shen and his sister—pitiful indeed.
